chunk_summary: Guidance on carbohydrate quality and fiber counseling for children emphasizes minimally processed staples, free sugar limits, fruit juice restrictions, and gradual fiber increases to support healthy growth and prevent complications.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of carbohydrate quality in pediatric nutrition and energy balance.
Learn WHO and AAP guidelines for free sugar intake and fruit juice consumption in children.
Recognize strategies for fiber introduction and management of intolerance or constipation.
Identify appropriate beverage choices and avoid non-sugar sweeteners for weight control.
key_facts: Carbohydrates should supply roughly 50% of total energy for children, prioritizing minimally processed staples like whole grains and pulses.
WHO recommends free sugars below 10% of energy, with additional benefits below 5%.
AAP advises no fruit juice for children under 12 months, and limits of 120 mL/day for ages 1–3 years and 120–180 mL/day for ages 4–6 years.
Energy drinks should be avoided entirely in children and adolescents.
Fiber should be increased gradually with water to prevent constipation, using fruits, vegetables, pulses, and whole grains.
reference_quote: The WHO sugars guideline recommends keeping free sugars below 10% of energy, with additional benefit below 5%.
document_key_topics: Free sugar intake and beverage counseling
